skill.name: find-bugs
skill.description: Find bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues in local branch changes. Use when asked to review changes, find bugs, security review, or audit code on the current branch.
skill.summary: Structured branch review for bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues.
skill.author: getsentry

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged one external command and two weak cryptography patterns. Review confirms the git command is a legitimate read-only diff command for the skill purpose, and the cryptography findings are false positives in review checklist prose. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Read-only Git Command Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run `git diff master...HEAD` to inspect branch changes. This is aligned with a code review skill and uses a fixed command, but it still requires local repository command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is a fixed read-only Git diff used to gather review context. No user-controlled shell interpolation, network transfer, or destructive operation is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The line describes finding security vulnerabilities and code quality issues. It does not invoke or recommend any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is prose metadata, not executable code or cryptographic guidance. No weak algorithm name or crypto operation is present in this line.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The line defines an output format field for File:Line reporting. It does not reference cryptographic code or an algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a reporting template line. It contains no cryptographic primitive, no hashing instruction, and no executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references `git diff master...HEAD` for branch review context. The command is fixed and read-only, so the residual risk is limited to normal local command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is semantically required for branch diff review and has no dynamic arguments in the skill text. It is still documented as an external command pattern.

content.value_statement: Branch reviews can miss security defects, logic bugs, and untested edge cases. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured local diff review.
actual_capabilities: Requests the full branch diff with `git diff master...HEAD` before reviewing.
Maps user inputs, database queries, authentication checks, external calls, and cryptographic operations.
Checks common security classes such as injection, XSS, CSRF, IDOR, races, and information disclosure.
Verifies potential issues against surrounding code and existing tests before reporting.
Produces findings with file, line, severity, problem, evidence, fix, and references.
Separates significant bugs and security issues from style-only feedback.
limitations: It depends on the assistant having access to the local repository and branch diff.
It does not make code changes or apply fixes by itself.
It may need extra file reads when the diff output is truncated.
It cannot prove runtime behavior without tests, logs, or execution context.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge Security Review
use_cases.description: Review a feature branch for exploitable defects before it reaches the main branch.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Bug Sweep
use_cases.description: Find likely logic bugs, missing authorization checks, and risky edge cases in local changes.

use_cases.title: Release Risk Audit
use_cases.description: Check a release branch for security and quality risks that need attention before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Branch Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the find-bugs skill to review my current branch for security vulnerabilities, bugs, and code quality issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of local branch changes

prompt_templates.title: Focused Security Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this branch with find-bugs and focus on authentication, authorization, injection, CSRF, and information disclosure risks.

prompt_templates.title: Backend Risk Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use find-bugs to inspect changed backend code. Map inputs, database queries, external calls, and state changes before listing findings.

prompt_templates.title: High-confidence Release Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full find-bugs review on this release branch. Read every changed file, verify each suspected issue, and list unverified areas separately.

output_examples.input: Review my current branch for security problems.
output: Reviewed the full branch diff and the changed files.
Found one medium authorization issue where an update path checks authentication but not resource ownership.
Recommended adding an ownership check and a regression test for cross-account access.

output_examples.input: Find bugs in this pull request before I merge it.
output: Mapped inputs, database queries, session changes, and external calls in the modified files.
No critical or high findings were identified.
One low-risk test gap remains around a numeric boundary condition.

output_examples.input: Audit this branch and tell me what you could not verify.
output: Completed the checklist for each changed file.
Could not verify production rate limits because configuration was not present in the repository.
Reported the gap separately from confirmed findings.

best_practices: Run the skill from the repository root with the target branch checked out.
Provide test results or relevant runtime context when available.
Ask for verification of each finding before deciding on remediation work.
anti_patterns: Using the skill as a replacement for tests, static analysis, or human review.
Asking for style-only feedback when the skill is designed for bugs and security issues.
Reviewing a partial diff and treating the results as a complete audit.

faq.question: Does this skill modify code?
faq.answer: No. The skill tells the assistant to report findings and wait for the user to decide what to fix.

faq.question: Why does it ask for `git diff master...HEAD`?
faq.answer: It uses the branch diff to identify changed code that needs security and bug review.

faq.question: Can it review security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes checks for injection, XSS, authentication, authorization, CSRF, race conditions, sessions, cryptography, disclosure, and denial of service.

faq.question: Will it report style issues?
faq.answer: No. The instructions explicitly skip stylistic and formatting issues.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the repository, branch context, relevant tests, and any areas that deserve special attention.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
